### Set Sandbox Timeout (Python)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.3.3/sandbox_sync/page.mdx

Sets or updates the timeout for a specified sandbox. The timeout determines how long a sandbox can remain active before being automatically terminated. This function can be used to both increase and decrease the existing timeout. Note that there are limits on the maximum duration a sandbox can be kept alive, varying by user subscription tier.

```python
from typing import Optional

# Assuming SandboxApiBase and SandboxInfo are defined elsewhere

class SandboxApi:
    @staticmethod
    @overload
    def set_timeout(sandbox_id: str,
                    timeout: int,
                    api_key: Optional[str] = None,
                    domain: Optional[str] = None,
                    debug: Optional[bool] = None,
                    request_timeout: Optional[float] = None) -> None:
        """Set the timeout of the sandbox specified by sandbox ID."""
        pass
```

--------------------------------

### Set Timeout for Specified Sandbox

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.0.6/sandbox_async/page.mdx

Sets the timeout for a specified sandbox using its ID. This method allows for dynamic adjustment of the sandbox's lifespan. Note that there are maximum limits on how long a sandbox can be kept alive, varying for Pro and Hobby users.

```APIDOC
## POST /sandboxes/{sandbox_id}/timeout

### Description
Sets the timeout for a specified sandbox using its ID. This method allows for dynamic adjustment of the sandbox's lifespan. Note that there are maximum limits on how long a sandbox can be kept alive, varying for Pro and Hobby users.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/sandboxes/{sandbox_id}/timeout

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
- **sandbox_id** (string) - Required - The ID of the sandbox.

#### Query Parameters
- **timeout** (integer) - Required - Timeout for the sandbox in seconds. Maximum 86400 seconds for Pro users and 3600 seconds for Hobby users.
- **api_key** (string) - Optional - API key for authentication.
- **domain** (string) - Optional - Domain for the sandbox.
- **debug** (boolean) - Optional - Enable debug mode.
- **request_timeout** (float) - Optional - Timeout for the request in seconds.

### Request Example
```json
{
  "timeout": 7200,
  "api_key": "your_api_key",
  "domain": "example.com",
  "debug": true,
  "request_timeout": 120.0
}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **message** (string) - Indicates the timeout was successfully set.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "message": "Sandbox timeout successfully updated."
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Set Sandbox Timeout Instance (Python)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.5.1/sandbox_async/page.mdx

Sets or updates the timeout for the current sandbox instance. The sandbox will be automatically killed once the timeout expires. This method can be used to extend or reduce the existing timeout. It requires the new timeout value in seconds and optionally accepts a request timeout.

```python
await sandbox.set_timeout(timeout: int,
                      request_timeout: Optional[float] = None) -> None
```

--------------------------------

### Set Sandbox Timeout (Python)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.0.2/sandbox_async/page.mdx

Sets or updates the timeout for the current sandbox instance. The sandbox will be automatically killed when the timeout expires. This method can adjust the sandbox's lifespan. It takes the timeout duration in seconds and an optional request timeout.

```python
@overload
async def set_timeout(timeout: int,
                      request_timeout: Optional[float] = None) -> None:
    """
    Set the timeout of the sandbox.

    After the timeout expires the sandbox will be automatically killed.
    This method can extend or reduce the sandbox timeout set when creating the sandbox or from the last call to `.set_timeout`.

    Maximum time a sandbox can be kept alive is 24 hours (86_400 seconds) for Pro users and 1 hour (3_600 seconds) for Hobby users.

    **Arguments**:
        - timeout: Timeout for the sandbox in **seconds**
        - request_timeout: Timeout for the request in **seconds**
    """
    pass
```

--------------------------------

### Set Sandbox Timeout (Instance Method) (Python)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.7.1/sandbox_sync/page.mdx

Sets the timeout for the current sandbox instance. The sandbox will be automatically killed after the specified timeout in seconds. This method can adjust the existing timeout. It takes the timeout duration and an optional request timeout. Maximum sandbox lifetime is 24 hours for Pro users and 1 hour for Hobby users.

```python
@overload
def set_timeout(timeout: int, request_timeout: Optional[float] = None) -> None:
    """Set the timeout of the sandbox."""
    # Implementation details...
```

### Set Sandbox Timeout

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.0.4/sandbox/page.mdx

Sets the timeout for a specified sandbox. The sandbox will be automatically killed after the timeout expires. This can extend or reduce the existing timeout. Returns a Promise that resolves when the timeout is set.

```typescript
await Sandbox.setTimeout(sandboxId, timeoutMs)

```

--------------------------------

### Set Sandbox Timeout (Python)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.10.0/sandbox_async/page.mdx

Asynchronously sets or updates the timeout for a sandbox instance. The sandbox will be automatically killed when the timeout expires. Accepts the new timeout in seconds and an optional request timeout. This method can be used to dynamically adjust sandbox lifespans.

```python
@overload
async def set_timeout(timeout: int,
                      request_timeout: Optional[float] = None) -> None:
    """
    Set the timeout of the sandbox.

    Arguments:
        timeout: Timeout for the sandbox in **seconds**
        request_timeout: Timeout for the request in **seconds**
    """
    pass
```

--------------------------------

### Set Sandbox Timeout (Python)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.0.5/sandbox_async/page.mdx

Allows setting or extending the timeout for a sandbox. The sandbox will be automatically killed when the timeout expires. Maximum sandbox lifespan is restricted to 24 hours for Pro users and 1 hour for Hobby users. Arguments include the timeout duration in seconds and an optional request timeout.

```python
async def set_timeout(timeout: int,
                      request_timeout: Optional[float] = None) -> None
```

```python
async def set_timeout(sandbox_id: str,
                      timeout: int,
                      api_key: Optional[str] = None,
                      domain: Optional[str] = None,
                      debug: Optional[bool] = None,
                      request_timeout: Optional[float] = None) -> None
```

--------------------------------

### Set E2B Sandbox Timeout (Python)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.5.6/sandbox_sync/page.mdx

Sets the timeout for an E2B sandbox. This method can extend or reduce the existing timeout. The maximum timeout is 24 hours for Pro users and 1 hour for Hobby users. It requires the timeout duration in seconds and optionally accepts a request timeout. This is an overload method.

```python
from typing import Optional

@overload
def set_timeout(timeout: int, request_timeout: Optional[float] = None) -> None:
    """Set the timeout of the sandbox."""
    pass
```

--------------------------------

### Set Sandbox Timeout

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.3.3/sandbox_async/page.mdx

Sets the timeout for a sandbox. This can extend or reduce the existing timeout. The sandbox will be automatically killed after the timeout expires.

```APIDOC
## POST /sandboxes/{sandbox_id}/set_timeout

### Description
Sets the timeout of the specified sandbox. After the timeout expires the sandbox will be automatically killed. This method can extend or reduce the sandbox timeout set when creating the sandbox or from the last call to `.set_timeout`. Maximum time a sandbox can be kept alive is 24 hours (86_400 seconds) for Pro users and 1 hour (3_600 seconds) for Hobby users.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/sandboxes/{sandbox_id}/set_timeout

#### Path Parameters
- **sandbox_id** (string) - Required - Sandbox ID

#### Query Parameters
- **timeout** (integer) - Required - Timeout for the sandbox in seconds
- **request_timeout** (float) - Optional - Timeout for the request in seconds
- **api_key** (string) - Optional - API key to use for authentication
- **domain** (string) - Optional - Domain to use for the request
- **debug** (boolean) - Optional - Enable debug mode

### Request Example
```json
{
  "timeout": 3600,
  "request_timeout": 60.0
}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **None** - This endpoint does not return a response body on success.

#### Response Example
(No response body)
```

--------------------------------

```APIDOC

```

Allows setting or updating the timeout for a sandbox. The sandbox will be automatically killed when the timeout expires. This can be used to extend or reduce the initially set timeout. Maximum timeout is 24 hours for Pro users and 1 hour for Hobby users.

/sandboxes/{sandbox_id}/set-timeout

- **request_timeout** (float) - Optional - Timeout for the request in seconds.

### Set Sandbox Timeout in Python

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v2.0.1/sandbox_async/page.mdx

Sets the timeout for a specified sandbox. The sandbox will be automatically killed after the timeout expires. This can be used to extend or reduce the existing timeout. The maximum timeout is 24 hours for Pro users and 1 hour for Hobby users.

```python
import asyncio
from typing import Optional, List
from datetime import datetime

from e2b.api_client import ApiParams
from e2b.sandbox.sandbox import SandboxInfo, SandboxMetrics


@overload
@staticmethod
async def set_timeout(sandbox_id: str, timeout: int,
                      **opts: Unpack[ApiParams]) -> None

@class_method_variant("_cls_set_timeout")
async def set_timeout(timeout: int, **opts: Unpack[ApiParams]) -> None


@overload
async def get_info(**opts: Unpack[ApiParams]) -> SandboxInfo

@overload
@staticmethod
async def get_info(sandbox_id: str, **opts: Unpack[ApiParams]) -> SandboxInfo

@class_method_variant("_cls_get_info")
async def get_info(**opts: Unpack[ApiParams]) -> SandboxInfo


@overload
async def get_metrics(start: Optional[datetime.datetime] = None,
                      end: Optional[datetime.datetime] = None,
                      **opts: Unpack[ApiParams]) -> List[SandboxMetrics]

@overload
@staticmethod
async def get_metrics(sandbox_id: str,
                      start: Optional[datetime.datetime] = None,
                      end: Optional[datetime.datetime] = None,
                      **opts: Unpack[ApiParams]) -> List[SandboxMetrics]

@class_method_variant("_cls_get_metrics")
async def get_metrics(start: Optional[datetime.datetime] = None,
                      end: Optional[datetime.datetime] = None,
                      **opts: Unpack[ApiParams]) -> List[SandboxMetrics]
```
